workflow_rationale: Greenfield project — every module, configuration file, and directory structure must be created from scratch. There is no existing codebase to refactor or migrate. The entire application is a new feature delivering end-to-end payroll automation.

phases.name: Project Foundation
phases.description: Create project scaffolding: package.json with pinned dependencies, environment config template, .gitignore, directory structure, and the shared Winston logger that all other modules depend on.

subtasks.description: Create package.json with pinned dependencies (axios@1.15.0 exact), .env.example with all 14 environment variables, update .gitignore to exclude node_modules/.env/logs/output/sessions, and create directory structure with .gitkeep files (src/auth/, src/esocial/, src/notifications/, src/jobs/, src/utils/, config/, logs/, output/guias/).

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const pkg = require('./package.json'); console.log(pkg.dependencies.axios === '1.15.0' ? 'AXIOS_PINNED_OK' : 'AXIOS_PIN_FAIL'); console.log(pkg.engines.node === '>=18' ? 'ENGINES_OK' : 'ENGINES_FAIL');"

subtasks.description: Create src/utils/logger.js — Winston logger with DailyRotateFile transport (30d retention, app-%DATE%.log pattern), colored console transport, configurable LOG_LEVEL and LOG_DIR via env vars, mandatory .on('error') handler for DailyRotateFile v5.0.0+, and format: [timestamp] [LEVEL] [context] message. Never log sensitive data.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && npm install --ignore-scripts 2>&1 | tail -1 && node -e "const logger = require('./src/utils/logger'); logger.info('Test message'); console.log('LOGGER_OK');"

phases.name: Gov.br Authentication & HTTP Client
phases.description: Build the Playwright-based gov.br headless login with session capture and persistence, plus the Axios HTTP client factory with auto-reauth interceptor for eSocial API calls.

subtasks.description: Create src/auth/govbr.js — Playwright headless authentication module. authenticate() function: launch Chromium headless, navigate to login.esocial.gov.br, fill CPF/password from env vars, submit, wait for redirect, capture session via context.storageState(). Persist session to session.json for reuse across restarts. Build cookie string for Axios. Handle CAPTCHA/2FA detection (log + abort). Restore existing session.json if valid. Export { authenticate }.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const { authenticate } = require('./src/auth/govbr'); console.log(typeof authenticate === 'function' ? 'AUTH_MODULE_OK' : 'AUTH_MODULE_FAIL');"

subtasks.description: Create src/esocial/client.js — Axios HTTP client factory. createClient(session) function: create Axios instance with baseURL https://login.esocial.gov.br, 30s timeout, Cookie header built from session.cookies array. Response interceptor catches 401/403, uses _retried flag to prevent infinite loops, calls authenticate() for re-auth, updates both error.config.headers.Cookie AND instance.defaults.headers.Cookie with new session. Export { createClient }.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const { createClient } = require('./src/esocial/client'); console.log(typeof createClient === 'function' ? 'CLIENT_MODULE_OK' : 'CLIENT_MODULE_FAIL');"

phases.name: Core eSocial Business Logic
phases.description: Implement the core payroll operations: listing open payrolls, verifying competency status, closing payroll, generating DAE slip, and downloading the PDF.

subtasks.description: Create src/esocial/folha.js — Payroll management module. Functions: listarFolhasAbertas(client) returns array of open payrolls; verificarCompetencia(client, competencia) returns competency status; encerrarFolha(client, competencia) closes payroll with graceful handling if already closed (log + skip). Auto-calculate competencia from current date when COMPETENCIA_MES/ANO is 'auto' (previous month, handling Jan→Dec year rollback). All operations logged, no sensitive data exposed. Export { listarFolhasAbertas, verificarCompetencia, encerrarFolha }.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const m = require('./src/esocial/folha'); const fns = ['listarFolhasAbertas','verificarCompetencia','encerrarFolha']; const ok = fns.every(f => typeof m[f] === 'function'); console.log(ok ? 'FOLHA_MODULE_OK' : 'FOLHA_MODULE_FAIL');"

subtasks.notes: Created src/esocial/folha.js with listarFolhasAbertas, verificarCompetencia, encerrarFolha. Auto-competência calculation handles Jan→Dec rollback. Graceful already-closed handling. All exports verified.

subtasks.description: Create src/esocial/guia.js — DAE generation and PDF download module. Functions: gerarGuia(client, competencia) triggers DAE slip generation, returns guia ID; downloadGuiaPDF(client, guiaId, outputPath) downloads PDF to output/guias/DAE-MM-YYYY.pdf. Create output/guias/ directory if missing (fs.mkdirSync recursive). Check file existence before download to prevent duplicates. Validate downloaded file is non-empty. Export { gerarGuia, downloadGuiaPDF }.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const m = require('./src/esocial/guia'); const fns = ['gerarGuia','downloadGuiaPDF']; const ok = fns.every(f => typeof m[f] === 'function'); console.log(ok ? 'GUIA_MODULE_OK' : 'GUIA_MODULE_FAIL');"

phases.name: Notification Channels
phases.description: Build the SMTP email notification module with PDF attachment support and the WhatsApp Web client with persistent session and QR code pairing.

subtasks.description: Create src/notifications/email.js — SMTP email notification module using Nodemailer. Functions: createTransporter() creates SMTP transporter from env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS), calls transporter.verify() at init to validate connection; sendEmail({ subject, body, attachments }) sends email to EMAIL_TO with HTML body, supports PDF attachment for DAE. Both success and failure notification templates. All SMTP errors caught and logged (best-effort, never abort job). Export { initEmail, sendEmail }.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const m = require('./src/notifications/email'); console.log(typeof m.sendEmail === 'function' ? 'EMAIL_MODULE_OK' : 'EMAIL_MODULE_FAIL');"

subtasks.description: Create src/notifications/whatsapp.js — WhatsApp Web notification module using whatsapp-web.js. Functions: initWhatsApp() initializes Client with LocalAuth strategy, session stored in .wwebjs_auth/. Display QR code via qrcode-terminal on first run. Handle events: 'qr' (display QR), 'ready' (log ready), 'disconnected' (log + re-display QR), 'auth_failure' (log error). Configure puppeteer to reuse Playwright's Chromium binary via executablePath to save ~400MB disk. sendWhatsApp(number, message) sends message to configured number. Export { initWhatsApp, sendWhatsApp, getWhatsAppClient }.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const m = require('./src/notifications/whatsapp'); console.log(typeof m.initWhatsApp === 'function' && typeof m.sendWhatsApp === 'function' ? 'WHATSAPP_MODULE_OK' : 'WHATSAPP_MODULE_FAIL');"

phases.name: Job Orchestration & Entry Point
phases.description: Build the monthly job orchestrator that sequences auth → close → DAE → notify with dual-channel error handling, plus the application entry point with cron scheduling, WhatsApp init, and --run-now CLI support.

subtasks.description: Create src/jobs/monthly.js — Job orchestrator module. Functions: runJob() executes full sequence: (1) acquire file lock /tmp/esocial-auto.lock (fs.writeFileSync flag:'wx', with PID for stale detection), (2) authenticate via govbr, (3) create HTTP client, (4) calculate competencia (auto from date or env), (5) list/verify/close payroll, (6) generate DAE + download PDF, (7) send success email with PDF attachment, (8) send WhatsApp success message, (9) release lock in finally. On ANY error: best-effort notification on both channels (each in own try/catch), then re-throw. setupCron() configures node-cron schedule from CRON_SCHEDULE or JOB_DIA_FECHAMENTO env var with timezone America/Sao_Paulo, validates expression at startup with cron.validate(). getCompetencia() auto-calculates previous month handling Jan→Dec rollback. Export { runJob, setupCron, getCompetencia }.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const m = require('./src/jobs/monthly'); const fns = ['runJob','setupCron','getCompetencia']; const ok = fns.every(f => typeof m[f] === 'function'); console.log(ok ? 'MONTHLY_MODULE_OK' : 'MONTHLY_MODULE_FAIL');"

subtasks.description: Create index.js — Application entry point. Load dotenv first. Parse --run-now CLI flag from process.argv. Initialize WhatsApp client (await 'ready' event). On WhatsApp 'ready': set up cron job via setupCron(). If --run-now: immediately call runJob() then process.exit(). Handle unhandled rejections and uncaught exceptions (log + notify). Log startup info (schedule, competencia target, Node.js version). Validate required env vars at startup (GOVBR_CPF, GOVBR_SENHA minimum). Validate cron expression at startup.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "try { require('./index.js'); } catch(e) { console.log(e.message.includes('GOVBR_CPF') || e.message.includes('Missing') || e.message.includes('required') ? 'INDEX_VALIDATES_ENV_OK' : 'INDEX_FAIL: ' + e.message); }"
verification.expected: INDEX_VALIDATES_ENV_OK printed (fails gracefully due to missing .env, confirming validation works)

phases.name: Integration & Hardening
phases.description: Verify all modules wire together correctly. Test npm install, npm run setup, module imports chain, CLI flags. Ensure no circular dependencies, all exports match expected API, and edge cases are handled.

subtasks.description: End-to-end integration verification: (1) Verify npm install completes without errors. (2) Verify all 8 source modules load without circular dependency errors (require each module). (3) Verify --run-now flag is parsed correctly. (4) Verify .gitignore excludes node_modules, .env, logs/, output/, session.json, .wwebjs_auth/. (5) Verify package.json has correct scripts (start, dev, setup, run-now). (6) Verify axios is pinned to exactly 1.15.0. (7) Verify directory structure matches spec. (8) Fix any integration issues found.

verification.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && npm install --ignore-scripts 2>&1 | tail -3 && node -e "const modules = ['./src/utils/logger','./src/auth/govbr','./src/esocial/client','./src/esocial/folha','./src/esocial/guia','./src/notifications/email','./src/notifications/whatsapp','./src/jobs/monthly']; let ok=true; modules.forEach(m => { try { require(m); } catch(e) { console.error('FAIL:', m, e.message); ok=false; } }); console.log(ok ? 'ALL_MODULES_OK' : 'MODULES_HAVE_ERRORS');"

implementation_order: Phase 2: Authentication (govbr.js, client.js) — depends on Phase 1
Phase 3: Business Logic (folha.js, guia.js) — depends on Phase 2
Phase 4: Notifications (email.js, whatsapp.js) — depends on Phase 1 ONLY (parallel with Phase 2+3)
Phase 5: Orchestration (monthly.js, index.js) — depends on Phase 3 AND Phase 4

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-1-foundation. Phase 2 creates auth/esocial modules while Phase 4 creates notification modules — completely different file sets with no overlap.
Phase 3 depends on Phase 2 (not Phase 4) and Phase 4 depends on Phase 1 (not Phase 2/3). If Phase 4 is still in progress when Phase 3 starts, they can run concurrently.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.4x faster than sequential — Phases 2+4 run in parallel, then Phase 3 may overlap with Phase 4 tail

acceptance_criteria: All 12 project files created and functional
All 8 source modules load without circular dependency errors
axios pinned to exactly 1.15.0 (npm ls axios confirms)
No sensitive data (CPF, password, tokens) appears in any log output
Each module exports the expected public API functions
Logger writes to both console and logs/ directory
package.json scripts (start, dev, setup, run-now) are correctly configured

verification_steps.name: Module Load Test
verification_steps.expected_outcome: ALL_OK — all modules load without errors

verification_steps.name: Dependency Security Check
verification_steps.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && npm ls axios 2>/dev/null | grep axios && npm audit --json 2>/dev/null | node -e "const d=require('fs').readFileSync('/dev/stdin','utf8'); const a=JSON.parse(d); console.log('vulnerabilities:', a.metadata?.vulnerabilities?.critical || 0, 'critical');"

verification_steps.name: Secrets Scan in Source Files
verification_steps.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && grep -rn 'GOVBR_SENHA\|password.*=.*["'\'']\'\''\|senha.*=.*["'\'']\'\'' src/ index.js --include='*.js' | grep -v 'process.env' | grep -v '//' | head -5 || echo 'NO_HARDCODED_SECRETS'
verification_steps.expected_outcome: NO_HARDCODED_SECRETS — all secrets loaded from process.env only

verification_steps.name: Package.json Validation
verification_steps.command: cd /home/rlmit/workspace/esocial-auto && node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); console.log('axios:', p.dependencies.axios); console.log('engines:', p.engines.node); console.log('scripts:', Object.keys(p.scripts).join(','));"

verification_strategy.reasoning: High-risk due to handling government credentials (CPF, passwords, session tokens) and interacting with undocumented eSocial APIs. Security scan verifies no credentials leak in source code. Module load tests verify structural correctness without requiring live credentials. Full E2E testing requires real gov.br account and is manual.

unit_tests.notes: No automated test framework — unit verification via direct module execution. Live credential tests are manual.

integration_tests.notes: Full integration testing (auth → close → DAE → notify) requires valid gov.br credentials and SMTP setup. Structural integration (all modules load, no circular deps) is automated.

e2e_tests.notes: Requires live gov.br credentials, SMTP server, and paired WhatsApp. Cannot be automated in CI.

browser_verification.notes: No web UI — this is a CLI/headless background job

database_verification.notes: No database — file-based storage only (session.json, PDFs, logs)

checks: output/guias/ directory exists or is created at runtime
logs/ directory exists or is created at runtime
.env.example exists with all 14 environment variables documented
